description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body and slightly bulging sides, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two wide, dark metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood surface is weathered, showing a rich brown color with visible grain and signs of age, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements or frequent use. The top of the barrel is closed with a wooden lid, which features a small, round, dark plug or bung in the center, likely for filling or emptying contents. The metal hoops are fastened with visible rivets, providing structural reinforcement and a rustic appearance. There are no visible handles or spouts, and the barrel lacks any painted labels or decorative markings. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage yards, or near workshops, serving as a container for liquids or dry goods.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops and a closed wooden lid featuring a central plug. The surface is rustic brown with visible wood grain and no handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden lid with a central dark plug and the upper metal hoop clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the alignment of the metal hoops and a series of small rivets along the upper band.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the central plug on the lid visible and the wood grain pattern prominent.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the continuous metal hoops and the unmarked, weathered wood surface.
